key_insight: A confined-space CVD process with tailored precursors and thermal ramps enables deterministic growth of high-quality Mo(W)Te2 crystals and atomically sharp heterostructures.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable route to reproducible Te-based 2D heterointerfaces for quantum and topological device integration.
method: Confined-space chemical vapor deposition lowers the effective growth temperature window.
Tailored precursor configurations plus stepwise thermal ramps control crystal/alloy/heterostructure formation.
Aberration-corrected STEM, STM, and STS verify structure and electronic uniformity.
evidence_signal: High-resolution microscopy and spectroscopy show nearly atomically sharp, compositionally well-defined Mo(W)Te2 boundaries with distinct local density-of-states distributions.
watch_item: The approach still requires precise control of precursor placement and thermal profiling, so process sensitivity may affect reproducibility at scale.
paper_title: Controlled chemical vapor deposition for synthesis of emerging Mo(W)Te2 systems
